flows.title: From strategy to impact
flows.summary: Strategy breaks down into things you can measure: an objective decomposes into pillars and problems, each bet becomes a hypothesis or action, and every bet carries an impact contract against a target metric.

steps.label: Objective
steps.title: Start from the outcome
steps.description: A Core/Value node on the canvas is the KPI the team exists to move. Everything below it must eventually roll up here.

steps.label: Pillar / Problem
steps.title: Decompose into pillars and problems
steps.description: Groups on the canvas act as pillars: the named areas (and known problems) where the objective will be won or lost.

steps.label: Hypothesis / Action
steps.title: Place explicit bets
steps.description: Hypothesis and Action cards state what you believe and what you will do about it — each one owned, statused, and visible on the Strategy board.

steps.label: Metric impact
steps.title: Contract the expected impact
steps.description: An impact contract binds the bet to a target metric with direction, expected delta, baseline, and measurement window — measured deltas surface on Strategy chips and dashboard badges.

flows.title: From teams to dashboards
flows.summary: Workspace groups map to what each audience is allowed to see, and each group gets an operating view: an auto-derived group dashboard plus its own widget grid for review.

steps.label: Groups
steps.title: Model the org as groups
steps.description: Workspace groups (departments, squads) and canvas groups (pillars) define who the audiences are and which slice of the map belongs to them.

steps.label: Visibility & access
steps.title: Decide who sees what
steps.description: Access tags × groups × RLS gate every node and value — restricted metrics are masked with a badge, and dashboards only surface what the viewer may see.

steps.label: Operating views
steps.title: Give each audience its view
steps.description: Each canvas group derives a live dashboard (KPIs, health, series) automatically; the Layers panel and focus mode carve the same map into per-team working views.

steps.label: Dashboard / review
steps.title: Run the review from the dashboard
steps.description: Group dashboards carry their own widget grids; impact badges link each widget back to the strategy bets that target its metrics.

flows.title: From experimentation to knowledge
flows.summary: Every experiment closes its loop: a hypothesis drives an action, the action shows up as a metric signal, the signal is reviewed into an insight with evidence, and the evidence decides the next bet.

steps.label: Hypothesis
steps.title: State what you believe
steps.description: A Hypothesis card with assumptions and success criteria — connected on the canvas to the metrics it claims to move.

steps.label: Action / Experiment
steps.title: Run the experiment
steps.description: The Action card carries owner, status, and workflow; its impact contract fixes the measurement window before the work ships.

steps.label: Metric signal
steps.title: Read the signal
steps.description: Measured deltas against baseline appear on the bet’s chip: met, missed, or guardrail-breached — no data reads as unknown, never as zero.

steps.label: Insight / Evidence
steps.title: Capture what you learned
steps.description: Impact Review records won / lost / inconclusive and writes an evidence note — a notebook artifact attached to the card, with the numbers embedded.

steps.label: Next decision
steps.title: Let evidence pick the next bet
steps.description: Evidence stays attached to the map, so the next planning pass starts from what was actually learned, not from memory.

flows.title: From instrumentation to trust
flows.summary: A dashboard is only as good as its pipes: sources and events flow through tracking checks into catalogued metric definitions, quality status stays visible, and only then does a dashboard deserve trust.

steps.label: Source / Event
steps.title: Wire the source
steps.description: Source nodes bind warehouse queries, files, connectors, or manual series onto the canvas — the raw feed for every downstream metric.

steps.label: Tracking check
steps.title: Verify the tracking
steps.description: Stale or orphaned bindings are flagged on the node itself — a disconnected source shows a badge instead of silently zeroing data.

steps.label: Metric definition
steps.title: Catalog the definition
steps.description: Promoting a card to a Tracked Metric makes its name, formula, and values the shared definition every canvas placement follows.

steps.label: Quality / freshness
steps.title: Keep quality visible
steps.description: Freshness, alert rules, and connector run health surface as status on cards and in the Data hub — degraded data announces itself.

steps.label: Trusted dashboard
steps.title: Trust the dashboard
steps.description: Because widgets resolve from catalogued definitions and live bindings, the number in the review is the number in the pipe.

flows.title: From agent to operating map
flows.summary: Agents are first-class operators: through MCP and the API they read and update the same map the team uses, changes are verified against the live canvas, and results persist as evidence and dashboards.

steps.label: Agent request
steps.title: An agent asks for context
steps.description: Claude or any MCP client connects with a scoped key — the business map becomes structured context instead of copy-pasted docs.

steps.label: MCP / API
steps.title: Access through the same rules
steps.description: Every tool call is RLS-scoped, rate-limited, and audited — an agent can never see or change more than the user it acts for.

steps.label: Read / update map
steps.title: Read and build the tree
steps.description: Tools create metrics, relationships, and values, push series, and auto-layout — the canvas is a programmable surface.

steps.label: Verify change
steps.title: Verify against the live map
steps.description: Changes land on the shared canvas where the team (and its checks) see them immediately — same source of truth, no shadow copy.

steps.label: Evidence / dashboard
steps.title: Persist the result
steps.description: Agent work ends as durable artifacts: evidence notes on cards and metrics on dashboards, attributed and reviewable.

flows.title: Workflow orchestration
flows.summary: The operating cadence lives on the map: triggers and review rhythms route work to owners, metric reviews force decisions, and every decision produces the next owned work item.

steps.label: Trigger / cadence
steps.title: Something starts the loop
steps.description: An alert rule firing, a measurement window closing, or a standing review cadence — the map tells you when attention is due.

steps.label: Owner / action
steps.title: Work routes to an owner
steps.description: Cards carry owners, assignees, and workflow status; the Strategy board is the queue of who is doing what about which metric.

steps.label: Metric review
steps.title: Review against the numbers
steps.description: The review happens on the group dashboard and impact chips — expected vs measured, guardrails included — not in a slide deck.

steps.label: Decision
steps.title: Decide and record it
steps.description: Won, lost, inconclusive, keep measuring — the decision is captured where the work lives, with its evidence note.

steps.label: Next work item
steps.title: The loop produces the next item
steps.description: Each decision spawns the next hypothesis or action on the map, so the operating loop closes instead of trailing off.
